title: Dolphin Watching Boat Hire in Puerto Banus: Family Costs 2026
html:
Dolphin watching family boat hire in Puerto Banus starts at roughly EUR 350 for a private half-day, leaving from the marina at Puerto Jose Banus and tracking the resident pods off the Costa del Sol. BoatHire24 lists skippered boats that suit children, with calm morning departures and shaded seating.

Where to set off in Puerto Banus

Most family trips begin at Puerto Jose Banus, the headline marina between Marbella and Estepona. From the breakwater you can run east along the Golden Mile, where bottlenose dolphins are sighted year round.

  • Puerto Jose Banus: the main departure marina, close to the car park and restaurants.
  • Marbella (Puerto Deportivo): a short hop east, useful as an alternative pick-up.
  • Puerto Cabopino: a smaller marina near the dunes, good for quieter mornings.
  • Estepona Marina: west along the coast, often paired with longer cruises.
  • La Bajadilla: Marbella's working harbour, handy for early starts.

How likely are you to see dolphins on a family trip?

Sightings are common but never guaranteed, with bottlenose and striped dolphins seen on most calm mornings off Puerto Banus. A skipper who knows the local feeding lines improves the odds, and early departures give the flattest water. If you want to find a skippered charter at a relaxed family pace, you can filter by duration and guest count first. Trips usually run two to four hours.

Which boat to rent in Puerto Banus

For dolphin watching with children, choose a stable hull with shade and an easy boarding platform. The list below runs from nimble day boats up to a roomy motor yacht.

  1. RIB or small motorboat: best for short, nimble dolphin runs, seats up to 8, from EUR 250 a half-day.
  2. Day cruiser: a good all-rounder with cabin and shade, seats up to 10, from EUR 400.
  3. Sailing catamaran: steady and spacious for nervous swimmers, seats up to 12, from EUR 600 a day.
  4. Motor yacht: for a comfortable full day you can compare the Princess F70, which carries larger families in shaded comfort, from EUR 1,800.
  5. Crewed flybridge yacht: top-deck views and full crew, seats up to 12, from EUR 2,500 a day.

Once you have chosen a hull, booking a family boat is straightforward. You pick your date and group size, confirm with the operator, and pay online before the trip. Each listing shows capacity, included extras and whether a skipper comes as standard. If you prefer to weigh several options side by side, you can search available boats and compare price, length and departure marina in one place.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Puerto Banus?

A family dolphin trip ranges from about EUR 350 for a private half-day to EUR 2,500-plus for a crewed yacht over a full day. The figure depends on boat size, season and whether a skipper is included.

  • Shared dolphin tour: from around EUR 30 per adult in EUR.
  • Small motorboat half-day: from about EUR 250 in EUR.
  • Catamaran full day: from roughly EUR 600 in EUR.
  • Crewed yacht full day: from about EUR 2,500 in EUR, fuel often extra.

When is the best season for dolphin watching here?

The high season runs June to September, with the warmest, flattest mornings for spotting dolphins off the Costa del Sol. Spring and early autumn are quieter and still reliable. The local levante, an easterly wind, can push up a short chop by midday, so families book the first departure.

Aim for an 09:00 start in July or August: the sea off Puerto Banus is usually glassy then, the light is kind for photos, and you will be back before the afternoon levante builds and the day-trip crowds reach the marina.

In Spain, a licensed skipper can take you out without any boating qualification of your own, which is why most family dolphin trips are skippered. If you want to drive a small boat yourself, the operator confirms the exact requirements for that vessel before you book.

At a glance

  • Price band: from EUR 350 to EUR 2,500-plus per trip.
  • Peak months: June to September.
  • Trip length: two to four hours for a typical family outing.
  • Capacity: up to 12 guests on larger boats.
  • Currency: EUR.
  • Distance: dolphins are often found within 3 nautical miles of the marina.

Questions about boat rental in Puerto Banus

Is dolphin watching suitable for young children?

Yes, dolphin trips are well suited to families, as skippered boats stay close to the coast and keep to calm morning water. Operators carry child-size life jackets and adjust the pace for younger passengers.

How long does a typical family trip last?

A typical dolphin watching outing from Puerto Banus runs two to four hours, long enough to reach the open water beyond the marina and find the pods while keeping children comfortable and shaded.

Do we need to bring anything?

Bring sun cream, hats, water and a light layer for the breeze offshore, as the boat provides life jackets and shade. It is worth confirming with the operator whether snacks or fuel are included in the price.

Can we swim during the trip?

Many family charters include a swim stop in a sheltered bay along the coast, separate from the dolphin watching itself. The skipper chooses a calm anchorage so children can swim safely from the platform.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Puerto Banus or list your own, so a family can book a skippered dolphin trip for 2026 in minutes. To compare departure times, check availability and pick the morning slot that suits your group.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Puerto Banus and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
